name: NurbsSurfacePointList
summary: Provides access to the control points of a NURBS surface.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(NurbsSurfacePointList other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

params.name: other
params.doc: [Missing <param name="other"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Collections.NurbsSurfacePointList.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Collections.NurbsSurfacePointList,System.Double)"]

params.name: epsilon
params.doc: [Missing <param name="epsilon"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Collections.NurbsSurfacePointList.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Collections.NurbsSurfacePointList,System.Double)"]

methods.name: GetControlPoint
methods.signature: public ControlPoint GetControlPoint(int u, int v)
methods.summary: Gets the control point at the given (u, v) index.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

returns.doc: The control point at the given (u, v) index.

methods.name: GetGrevillePoint
methods.signature: public Point2d GetGrevillePoint(int u, int v)
methods.summary: Gets the 2-D Greville point associated with the control point at the given (u, v) index.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

returns.doc: A Surface UV coordinate on success, Point2d.Unset on failure.

methods.name: GetPoint(Int32, Int32, Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool GetPoint(int u, int v, out Point3d point)
methods.summary: Gets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given (u, v) index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetPoint(Int32, Int32, Point4d)
methods.signature: public bool GetPoint(int u, int v, out Point4d point)
methods.summary: Gets a homogeneous control point at the given (u, v) index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use the override that returns world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate and weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetWeight
methods.signature: public double GetWeight(int u, int v)
methods.summary: Gets the weight of a control point at the given (u, v) index. Note, if the surface is non-rational, the weight will be 1.0.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface V direction.

returns.doc: The control point weight if successful, Rhino.Math.UnsetValue otherwise.

methods.name: SetControlPoint(Int32, Int32, ControlPoint)
methods.signature: public bool SetControlPoint(int u, int v, ControlPoint cp)
methods.summary: Sets the control point at the given (u, v) index.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: cp
params.doc: The control point to set.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetControlPoint(Int32, Int32, Point3d)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use one of the SetPoint() overrides")] public bool SetControlPoint(int u, int v, Point3d cp)
methods.summary: Sets the control point at the given (u, v) index.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: cp
params.doc: The control point location to set (weight is assumed to be 1.0).

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Int32, Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int u, int v, Point3d point)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given (u, v) index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Int32, Point4d)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int u, int v, Point4d point)
methods.summary: Sets a homogeneous control point at the given (u, v) index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate and weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Int32, Point3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int u, int v, Point3d point, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point and weight at a given index. The 4-D representation is (x*w, y*w, z*w, w).

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinates of the control point.

params.name: weight
params.doc: Weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Int32, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int u, int v, double x, double y, double z)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given (u, v) index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: x
params.doc: X coordinate of control point.

params.name: y
params.doc: Y coordinate of control point.

params.name: z
params.doc: Z coordinate of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Int32, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int u, int v, double x, double y, double z, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets a homogeneous control point at the given (u, v) index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control point in the surface V direction.

params.name: x
params.doc: X coordinate of control point.

params.name: y
params.doc: Y coordinate of control point.

params.name: z
params.doc: Z coordinate of control point.

params.name: weight
params.doc: Weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetWeight
methods.signature: public bool SetWeight(int u, int v, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets the weight of a control point at the given (u, v) index. Note, if the surface is non-rational, it will be converted to rational.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface V direction.

params.name: weight
params.doc: The control point weight.

methods.name: UVNDirectionsAt
methods.signature: public bool UVNDirectionsAt(int u, int v, out Vector3d uDir, out Vector3d vDir, out Vector3d nDir)
methods.summary: Calculates the U, V, and N directions of a NURBS surface control point similar to the method used by Rhino's MoveUVN command.

params.name: u
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface U direction.

params.name: v
params.doc: Index of control-point along surface V direction.

params.name: uDir
params.doc: The U direction.

params.name: vDir
params.doc: The V direction.

params.name: nDir
params.doc: The N direction.

methods.name: ValidateSpacing
methods.signature: public bool ValidateSpacing(double closeTolerance, double stackTolerance, out IndexPair[] closeIndices, out IndexPair[] stackedIndices)
methods.summary: Simple check of distance between adjacent control points

properties.name: CountU
properties.summary: Gets the number of control points in the U direction of this surface.

properties.name: CountV
properties.summary: Gets the number of control points in the V direction of this surface.

properties.name: PointSize
properties.summary: Returns the control point size, or the number of doubles per control point. For rational curves, PointSize = Surface.Dimension + 1. For non-rational curves, PointSize = Surface.Dimension.
